methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Quantum Computing
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Quantum Computing
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Quantum Computing

data.narrative:
Nord Quantique is a seed-stage quantum computing company that has raised C$40M+ in total funding, including a C$23M round from Quantum Champions in December 2025. The company operates within a quantum computing sector characterized by a modest public comp universe of three traded entities (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and eight identifiable private peers including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

Traditional peer-median valuation analysis is not applicable to Nord Quantique due to insufficient datapoints at comparable stages and tiers. Within the seven publicly tracked quantum computing companies identified, none satisfy the simultaneous filtering criteria of matching seed stage, adjacent funding round, or similar valuation tier—a requirement of at least three comparable observations. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material bias and obscure the heterogeneity in quantum technology platforms, commercial readiness, and capital efficiency across the broader peer set.

Without reliable peer triangulation, valuation support must derive from alternative methods not detailed in the provided dataset. Key uncertainties include the absence of comparable seed-stage quantum firms with disclosed valuations, the wide divergence in technology approach and commercialization timelines across the identified private peers, and the limited precedent for standardized pricing in this nascent sector. Investors should rely on primary diligence on burn rate, IP defensibility, and pathway to differentiated applications rather than market multiples at this stage.
